dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.pause_pipeline_confirm: Pause the pipeline? Running jobs will finish, new jobs wait in the queue. Recommended before container stop.
dashboard.resume_pipeline_confirm: Resume the pipeline? Queued jobs will be processed immediately.

logs.title: Logs
logs.retry_all_confirm: Really retry all jobs in 'error' state?
logs.retry_all_warnings_confirm: Really retry all jobs with warnings?
logs.cleanup_orphans_confirm: Scan all settled jobs and mark those whose file no longer exists as 'orphan'? (Status only — data preserved)
logs.cleanup_orphans_immich: Also check Immich assets? (Slower — makes API calls for each immich:* job)

job_detail.delete_confirm: Permanently delete job {key} and associated file?

duplicates.title: Duplicate Review
duplicates.batch_confirm: Auto-clean {n} exact duplicate group(s)? Only identical copies (SHA256) will be removed — similar images must be reviewed manually. Originals will be kept.
duplicates.keep_confirm: Keep {name}, delete all others in this group?
duplicates.not_duplicate_confirm: {name} is not a duplicate? The file will be re-processed.
duplicates.merge_all_confirm: Transfer missing metadata from {n} files to {target}?

settings.title: Settings
settings.inboxes_desc: Monitored folders from which files are automatically picked up and processed. Each directory can be individually enabled/disabled and configured with its own options (e.g. Immich upload, folder tags, dry run).
settings.label: Label
settings.ai_desc: Automatically classifies images and videos using AI (type, tags, description, quality, NSFW). Uses an LM Studio / OpenAI-compatible backend. The AI can supplement or correct static sorting rules.
settings.ai_image_resize_desc: Resizes images before AI analysis to the specified maximum size. Recommended for local models (e.g. LM Studio) — drastically reduces payload and speeds up processing without noticeable quality loss.
settings.api_key_hint: Leave empty to keep existing key
settings.ai2_desc: Second OpenAI-compatible backend for parallel processing. When configured, the currently idle backend is used automatically.
settings.prompt_hint: The prompt controls how the AI analyzes images. Response must be valid JSON.
settings.geo_desc: Converts GPS coordinates from EXIF data into readable place names (country, city, suburb). These are written as keywords into the file and used as tags in Immich.
settings.dup_desc: Detects identical files (SHA256) and visually similar images (perceptual hash). Exact duplicates are automatically skipped, similar images go to duplicate review for manual inspection.
settings.dup_raw_jpg_pair_hint: Off = pairs go to duplicate review for manual selection
settings.ocr_desc: Text recognition in images via AI backend. Detected text is written as UserComment into the file and the keyword 'OCR' is added. Requires an active AI backend.
settings.ocr_always: All images (including photos with signs, menus etc.)
settings.ocr_hint: "Smart" skips OCR for regular photos, "All images" also detects text on signs, posters etc.
settings.ocr_hint2: Note: OCR requires an active AI backend (AI Analysis must be enabled).
settings.ordner_tags_desc: Automatically generates keywords and Immich albums from the inbox folder structure.
settings.ordner_tags_hint: This module must be enabled globally here AND the 'Folder Tags' checkbox on the respective inbox directory must also be checked.
settings.ordner_tags_detail:
A file in /inbox/Vacation/Mallorca/ gets:
• The EXIF keywords Vacation, Mallorca and Vacation Mallorca
• In Immich the album Vacation Mallorca and corresponding tags

Both settings (module + inbox checkbox) are checked at runtime — changes take effect immediately, even for jobs already in the queue.
settings.inbox_options_info:
Immich — Upload files to Immich after processing instead of the local library. Applied when the job is created.

Folder Tags — Generates EXIF keywords and Immich albums from the folder structure (e.g. Vacation/Mallorca/ → tags 'Vacation', 'Mallorca' + album 'Vacation Mallorca'). Also requires the global Folder Tags module. Checked at runtime — changes take effect immediately, even for queued jobs.

Dry Run — Files are analysed but not moved or uploaded. Useful for testing. Applied when the job is created.

Active — Only active directories are monitored by the filewatcher. Applied on the next scan cycle.
settings.smtp_desc: Sends an email when processing errors occur. Useful for early detection of problems, e.g. when the AI backend is unreachable or a file cannot be processed.
settings.immich_desc: Connects MediaAssistant to an Immich instance. Files from inbox directories with the 'Immich' flag enabled are automatically uploaded after processing, tagged, and optionally archived or locked (NSFW).
settings.immich_api_key_hint: API key from Immich: Account Settings → API Keys
settings.metadata_write_mode_desc: Controls how keywords, description and OCR text are stored. This affects both local file storage and Immich uploads.
settings.metadata_write_direct_hint: Keywords and description are written via ExifTool directly into the original file (IPTC/XMP depending on format). The file hash changes as a result. For Immich assets, the tagged file is uploaded as a new version and the original is replaced.
settings.metadata_write_sidecar_hint: The original file remains completely unchanged. Instead, a separate .xmp file is created next to the original (e.g. photo.jpg → photo.jpg.xmp). For new Immich uploads, the sidecar file is sent alongside the image. For existing Immich assets, no re-upload is performed — tags are only set via Immich API. Ideal for mobile app synchronization since the file hash does not change.
settings.immich_poll_desc: Detects new uploads in Immich (e.g. from mobile) and automatically processes them through the pipeline. Keywords, description and geodata are added. In 'Direct' mode the asset is replaced in Immich, in 'Sidecar' mode tags are only set via API.
settings.immich_users_desc: Each user needs their own API key for write access to their assets.
settings.immich_user_api_key_hint: API key from each user's Immich account: Account Settings → API Keys
settings.no_immich_users: No Immich users configured — the global API key will be used.
settings.iu_fields_required: Name and API key are required
settings.storage_desc: Defines the library base path and folder structure per category. Files are automatically moved to the matching subfolder after processing (except for Immich uploads, where the file is deleted after upload).
settings.filewatcher_desc: Monitors inbox directories for new files and automatically starts the processing pipeline. Can run continuously, within a time window, on a schedule, or manually only.
settings.appearance_desc: Language and color scheme of the web interface. Changes take effect immediately after saving.
settings.video_thumbnails_desc: Extracts evenly distributed frames from videos via ffmpeg so the AI can analyze video content. Without this option, videos are only classified based on metadata (EXIF, filename).
settings.video_thumbnail_frames_hint: Evenly distributed across video duration (1–50). Lower resolution = more frames possible.
settings.video_thumbnail_scale_hint: Percent of original resolution. 50% at 4K = 1080p, at 1080p = 540p.
settings.manual_hint: In manual mode, processing only runs on demand (Dashboard → Scan now).
settings.sorting_rules_desc: Static rules for classifying files based on filename, extension or EXIF data. Rules are evaluated before AI analysis — the first matching rule determines the category. The AI can then supplement or correct the result.
settings.sorting_rules_hint: Rules are evaluated top to bottom — the first matching rule determines the target category. When AI analysis is enabled, it can improve the result: e.g. rescue a personal photo from «Sourceless» or «Unknown» when the static rule was too broad.
settings.sorting_rules_help:
Filename contains — Checks if the text appears in the filename (case insensitive).
Example: -WA matches IMG-20240101-WA0012.jpg

Filename pattern (Regex) — Regular expression matched against the full filename (case insensitive). Allows defining complex patterns.
Key regex building blocks:
. = any character   .* = any number of characters   \d = a digit (0–9)
[0-9a-f] = hex character   {4} = exactly 4 repetitions   ^ = start   $ = end
\. = a literal dot (must be escaped)   (a|b) = a or b   \w+ = one or more word characters
Examples:
• ^IMG_\d{8}_\d{6} — camera photos like IMG_20240315_143022.jpg
• ^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}\.\w+$ — WhatsApp UUID files like a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890.jpg
• ^Screenshot — files starting with "Screenshot"
• (meme|sticker|gif) — files containing "meme", "sticker" or "gif" in the name

File extension — One or more extensions, comma-separated (with or without dot).
Example: .mp4,.mov,.avi matches common video formats

EXIF Expression — Freely definable conditions on EXIF fields. Combine multiple conditions with & (AND) or | (OR).

Operators:
!= "" = field is not empty   == "" = field is empty
== value = field equals value   != value = field does not equal value
~ value = field contains value   !~ value = field does not contain value

Available fields: make, model, date, gps, gps_lat, gps_lon, software, width, height, file_type, mime_type, has_exif, duration, megapixel

Examples:
• make != "" & date != "" — Has camera manufacturer AND capture date (real camera photo)
• make ~ Apple — Camera manufacturer contains "Apple" (iPhone photos)
• make ~ Canon | make ~ Nikon — Canon OR Nikon camera
• gps != "" & make != "" — Has GPS data AND camera info
• software ~ WhatsApp — Images edited by WhatsApp
• has_exif == False — File has no EXIF data (messenger images, downloads)
• width != "" & height != "" & make == "" — Has resolution but no camera info (screenshot/download)
settings.rule_fields_required: Condition, value and target are required
settings.cat_fields_required: Key, label and path are required
settings.google_json_desc: Imports metadata from Google Takeout JSON sidecar files (photo.jpg.json). Missing capture dates, GPS coordinates and descriptions are automatically filled.
settings.google_json_hint: Fills missing EXIF data (date, GPS, description) from Google Photos JSON files. JSON files are automatically deleted after processing. Also detects Google duplicates like photo.jpg(1).json.
settings.dup_skip_confirm_hint: Disables the confirmation prompt for Keep, Delete, and "Not a duplicate" actions in the duplicate review

review.title: Review — Manual Classification
review.batch_confirm: Classify all review files as sourceless?

dryrun.title: Dry-Run Report
dryrun.subtitle: Preview of all files in dry-run mode
